01 - Data Exploration: GSE25066

Maria Szczerbinska

2026-06-28

Overview

This notebook performs initial exploration of GSE25066 - a microarray dataset of 508 primary breast tumours from tamoxifen-treated patients, with treatment response annotation (pathological complete response, pCR, vs. residual disease, RD).

Biological question: Are genes involved in the FGFR2-ERα signalling axis differentially expressed between tamoxifen-sensitive and tamoxifen-resistant tumours?

Genes of interest: FGFR2, ESR1, PGR, IRS1, BCL2L1

Data source: NCBI GEO, accession GSE25066
Platform: Affymetrix HGU133A microarray
Analysis method: limma (linear models for microarray data)

1. Setup

# Load required libraries
# Install if missing: BiocManager::install("limma"); BiocManager::install("GEOquery")

library(GEOquery)   # load GEO data
library(limma)      # differential expression for microarray
library(ggplot2)    # visualisation
library(dplyr)      # data manipulation

# Set ggplot2 theme for all plots
theme_set(theme_bw(base_size = 13))

2. Load data

# Load the downloaded series matrix file
gse <- getGEO(
  "GSE25066",
  destdir   = "../data/raw",
  GSEMatrix = TRUE
)

# Extract ExpressionSet object
eset <- gse[[1]]

# Basic dimensions
cat("Number of samples: ", ncol(eset),  "\n")
## Number of samples:  508
cat("Number of probes:  ", nrow(eset),  "\n")
## Number of probes:   22283

3. Explore clinical (phenotype) data

# Extract phenotype data (clinical annotations)
pheno <- pData(eset)

# Preview column names - we need to find tamoxifen response annotation
colnames(pheno)
##  [1] "title"                                   
##  [2] "geo_accession"                           
##  [3] "status"                                  
##  [4] "submission_date"                         
##  [5] "last_update_date"                        
##  [6] "type"

##  [8] "source_name_ch1"                         
##  [9] "organism_ch1"                            
## [10] "characteristics_ch1"                     
## [11] "characteristics_ch1.1"                   
## [12] "characteristics_ch1.2"                   
## [13] "characteristics_ch1.3"

## [15] "characteristics_ch1.5"

## [38] "label_protocol_ch1"

## [40] "hyb_protocol"

## [53] "contact_web_link"

## [56] "relation"                                
## [57] "age_years:ch1"                           
## [58] "chemosensitivity_prediction:ch1"         
## [59] "clinical_ajcc_stage:ch1"

## [62] "dlda30_prediction:ch1"                   
## [63] "drfs_1_event_0_censored:ch1"             
## [64] "drfs_even_time_years:ch1"                
## [65] "er_status_ihc_esr1_for indeterminate:ch1"

## [67] "erbb2_status:ch1"                        
## [68] "esr1_status:ch1"                         
## [69] "ggi_class:ch1"

## [71] "her2_status:ch1"                         
## [72] "pam50_class:ch1"                         
## [73] "pathologic_response_pcr_rd:ch1"          
## [74] "pathologic_response_rcb_class:ch1"       
## [75] "pr_status_ihc:ch1"                       
## [76] "rcb_0_i_prediction:ch1"                  
## [77] "sample id:ch1"                           
## [78] "set_class:ch1"                           
## [79] "source:ch1"                              
## [80] "tissue:ch1"                              
## [81] "type_taxane:ch1"
# GSE25066 contains treatment response as pCR (complete response)
# vs RD (residual disease) - key variable for our analysis

# Preview first few rows of relevant columns
head(pheno[, c(
  "title",
  "characteristics_ch1",
  "characteristics_ch1.1",
  "characteristics_ch1.2"
)])
##           title characteristics_ch1 characteristics_ch1.1 characteristics_ch1.2
## GSM615096  1002     sample id: 1002          source: ISPY       age_years: 37.8
## GSM615097  1005     sample id: 1005          source: ISPY       age_years: 45.8
## GSM615098  1009     sample id: 1009          source: ISPY       age_years: 40.7
## GSM615099  1011     sample id: 1011          source: ISPY       age_years: 40.8
## GSM615100  1016     sample id: 1016          source: ISPY       age_years: 35.5
## GSM615101  1019     sample id: 1019          source: ISPY       age_years: 52.2
# Find and tabulate tamoxifen response
# Note: column name may vary - adjust based on colnames() output above

# Common column name in GSE25066:
response_col <- "pathologic_response_pcr_rd:ch1"

response_table <- table(pheno[[response_col]])
print(response_table)
## 
##  NA pCR  RD 
##  20  99 389
# Plot sample distribution by response
ggplot(pheno, aes(x = pheno[[response_col]])) +
  geom_bar(fill = "steelblue") +
  labs(
    title = "Sample distribution by tamoxifen response",
    x     = "Treatment response",
    y     = "Number of samples",
    caption = "GSE25066 | n = 508"
  )
## Warning: Use of `pheno[[response_col]]` is discouraged.
## ℹ Use `.data[[response_col]]` instead.

ggsave(
  "../results/figures/01_sample_distribution.png",
  width = 6, height = 4, dpi = 150
)
## Warning: Use of `pheno[[response_col]]` is discouraged.
## ℹ Use `.data[[response_col]]` instead.

4. Quality control

# Expression value distribution across samples
# A well-normalised dataset should show similar distributions per sample

# Take a random subset of 40 samples for readability
set.seed(42)
sample_idx <- sample(ncol(eset), 40)

boxplot(
  exprs(eset)[, sample_idx],
  outline = FALSE,
  las     = 2,
  cex.axis = 0.5,
  col      = "steelblue",
  main     = "Expression distribution (random subset of 40 samples)",
  ylab     = "Expression value (log2)"
)

# Density plot of expression values - all samples overlaid
# All lines should roughly overlap if data is well-normalised

expr_df <- as.data.frame(exprs(eset)[, sample_idx]) |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols      = everything(),
    names_to  = "sample",
    values_to = "expression"
  )

ggplot(expr_df, aes(x = expression, group = sample)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.3, colour = "steelblue", linewidth = 0.3) +
  labs(
    title   = "Expression density (random subset of 40 samples)",
    x       = "Expression value (log2)",
    y       = "Density",
    caption = "GSE25066"
  )

ggsave(
  "../results/figures/01_expression_density.png",
  width = 7, height = 4, dpi = 150
)

5. PCA - overview of sample structure

# PCA helps detect batch effects and outliers
# and shows whether samples cluster by biological group

# Transpose: PCA expects samples as rows
pca_result <- prcomp(
  t(exprs(eset)),
  center = TRUE,
  scale. = FALSE
)

# Variance explained by each PC
var_explained <- round(
  summary(pca_result)$importance[2, 1:5] * 100, 1
)

# Build PCA dataframe for plotting
pca_df <- data.frame(
  PC1      = pca_result$x[, 1],
  PC2      = pca_result$x[, 2],
  response = pheno[[response_col]]
)

ggplot(pca_df, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, colour = response)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 2) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("pCR" = "#7BA7E0", "RD" = "#E07B7B", "NA" = "grey70")) +
  labs(
    title   = "PCA of GSE25066 samples",
    x       = paste0("PC1 (", var_explained[1], "% variance)"),
    y       = paste0("PC2 (", var_explained[2], "% variance)"),
    colour  = "Response",
    caption = "GSE25066 | n = 508"
  )

ggsave(
  "../results/figures/01_pca.png",
  width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 150
)

6. Genes of interest - quick look

# Check expression of key FGFR2-ERα pathway genes
# Note: microarray uses probe IDs, not gene symbols
# We need to map probe IDs to gene symbols via feature data

# Extract feature data (probe - gene symbol mapping)
fdata <- fData(eset)
head(fdata[, c("ID", "Gene Symbol", "Gene Title")])
##                  ID      Gene Symbol
## 1007_s_at 1007_s_at DDR1 /// MIR4640
## 1053_at     1053_at             RFC2
## 117_at       117_at            HSPA6
## 121_at       121_at             PAX8
## 1255_g_at 1255_g_at           GUCA1A
## 1294_at     1294_at MIR5193 /// UBA7
##                                                              Gene Title
## 1007_s_at discoidin domain receptor tyrosine kinase 1 /// microRNA 4640
## 1053_at                     replication factor C (activator 1) 2, 40kDa
## 117_at                             heat shock 70kDa protein 6 (HSP70B')
## 121_at                                                     paired box 8
## 1255_g_at                       guanylate cyclase activator 1A (retina)
## 1294_at   microRNA 5193 /// ubiquitin-like modifier activating enzyme 7
# Genes of interest
genes_of_interest <- c("FGFR2", "ESR1", "PGR", "IRS1", "BCL2L1")

# Find probes corresponding to these genes
probes_of_interest <- fdata[
  fdata[["Gene Symbol"]] %in% genes_of_interest,
  c("ID", "Gene Symbol")
]

print(probes_of_interest)
##                      ID Gene Symbol
## 203638_s_at 203638_s_at       FGFR2
## 203639_s_at 203639_s_at       FGFR2
## 204686_at     204686_at        IRS1
## 205225_at     205225_at        ESR1
## 206665_s_at 206665_s_at      BCL2L1
## 208225_at     208225_at       FGFR2
## 208228_s_at 208228_s_at       FGFR2
## 208229_at     208229_at       FGFR2
## 208234_x_at 208234_x_at       FGFR2
## 208305_at     208305_at         PGR
## 211233_x_at 211233_x_at        ESR1
## 211234_x_at 211234_x_at        ESR1
## 211235_s_at 211235_s_at        ESR1
## 211398_at     211398_at       FGFR2
## 211399_at     211399_at       FGFR2
## 211400_at     211400_at       FGFR2
## 211401_s_at 211401_s_at       FGFR2
## 211627_x_at 211627_x_at        ESR1
## 212312_at     212312_at      BCL2L1
## 215037_s_at 215037_s_at      BCL2L1
## 215551_at     215551_at        ESR1
## 215552_s_at 215552_s_at        ESR1
## 217163_at     217163_at        ESR1
## 217190_x_at 217190_x_at        ESR1
cat("\nFound", nrow(probes_of_interest), "probes for", 
    length(genes_of_interest), "genes of interest\n")
## 
## Found 24 probes for 5 genes of interest
